description:
Restore access to a corrupted VeraCrypt volume using an external header backup. 

Scenario: The encrypted volume '/home/ga/Volumes/critical_data.hc' has a corrupted volume header and cannot be mounted. A backup of the header is available at '/home/ga/Volumes/header_backup_critical.dat'.

Your tasks:
1. Use VeraCrypt to restore the volume header from the external backup file.
2. Mount the volume to '/home/ga/MountPoints/slot1' using the password 'DR-Restore#2024!'.
3. Locate the file 'RECOVERY_VERIFICATION.txt' inside the mounted volume.
4. Create a report at '/home/ga/Documents/recovery_report.txt'.
5. The first line of the report must be the verification code found in 'RECOVERY_VERIFICATION.txt'.
6. The subsequent lines must list all filenames found in the root of the mounted volume.
